description: Information stored using specific technical language, queried in casual/different phrasing. Tests whether semantic search can bridge the vocabulary gap that tag-based retrieval would miss.

memories.content: User's application implements rate limiting using a token bucket algorithm with a refill rate of 100 tokens per second and a burst capacity of 500 tokens.
The user's API gateway handles authentication via JWT tokens issued by Auth0. Token expiry is set to 1 hour with refresh tokens valid for 30 days.
User configured their CDN (CloudFront) with a 24-hour TTL for static assets and 5-minute TTL for API responses. Origin is an ALB in us-east-1.
User's monitoring stack uses Datadog for APM and logs, PagerDuty for alerting. Alert threshold for API latency is p99 > 500ms.

query: How do I throttle requests in my app? What are the limits I set up?

correct_epistemic_behavior: Correctly identify that 'throttle requests' refers to rate limiting
Provide the token bucket configuration details (100 tokens/sec, 500 burst)
Answer confidently since this is verbatim memory
Do not confuse JWT token expiry with rate limiting tokens
incorrect_behaviors: Failing to find rate limiting info because query says 'throttle' not 'rate limit'
Confusing JWT authentication tokens with rate limiting tokens
Expressing uncertainty when the information is clearly in verbatim memory
Providing CDN TTL info when asked about request throttling
